specialized_result_formatters.application/protein+fasta: Returns Protein sequences for queries in FASTA format
specialized_result_formatters.application/dna+jsonh+fasta: Returns DNA sequences for queries in JSONH-FASTA format
specialized_result_formatters.application/protein+jsonh+fasta: Returns Protein sequences for queries in JSONH-FASTA format

rql_operators.description: FIELD is not equal to VALUE
Text search accross string values in a data type
True for objects whose FIELD contains any of the provided values
ANDs two or more expressions together
ORs two ore more expressions together
Returns only the specified fields for query result objects
Sort results by field. Specify + or - to specify the sort direction
Limits the results to COUNT records starting at START
Retrieves the GenomeGroup from WORKSPACE_PATH for use in a query (e.g., &in(genome_id,GenomeGroup(/path/to/my/group)) )
Retrieves the FeatureGroup from WORKSPACE_PATH for use in a query (e.g., &in(feature_id,FeatureGroup(/path/to/my/group)) )

antibiotics.label: Antibiotics
antibiotics.description: Information about antibiotics, inlcuding their chemical and physical properties, pharmacology, and mechanism of action.

extra_query_examples.description: Query for antibiotics related to tuberculosis.  Return as JSON

version.description: Auto-generated by Apache Solr; increments whenever the record is updated, letting BV-BRC detect write conflicts and replicate safely.

antibiotic_name.description: Human-readable label chosen by curators; queryable without case sensitivity, so “cipro” and “Ciprofloxacin” match. Displayed in lists and reports.

atc_classification.description: One or more Anatomical Therapeutic Chemical codes describing therapeutic class and subgroup; stored as an array because a combination drug may map to several codes.

canonical_smiles.description: Deterministic SMILES string from PubChem. Enables exact structure search, fingerprint generation and 2-D depictions.

cas_id.description: Globally unique identifier from Chemical Abstracts Service; useful in regulatory forms and cross-database joins.

date_inserted.description: ISO-8601 UTC datetime stamped when the record entered BV-BRC; useful for change-logs and incremental downloads.

date_modified.description: Updates whenever any field changes; clients compare this to cached copies to know when to refresh.

description.description: One-to-three sentence plain-language summary of spectrum, indications or notable traits. Stored as an array so sentence boundaries are preserved.

drugbank_interactions.description: DrugBank IDs or names with clinically relevant interactions (synergy, antagonism, adverse). Displayed in AMR reports and pharmacology dashboards.

inchi_key.description: Fixed-length hash of the full InChI; ideal for rapid deduplication and web look-ups.

isomeric_smiles.description: SMILES that includes chiral and isotopic information; required for 3-D conformer generation or docking.

mechanism_of_action.description: Curated phrases describing biochemical target(s) and effect (e.g. “Inhibits cell-wall transpeptidase”). Multiple entries allowed for multi-target drugs.

molecular_formula.description: Elemental composition in standard order (C, H, then alphabetical). Used by cheminformatics tools for mass and isotope calculations.

molecular_weight.description: Calculated from the formula. Stored as a string to avoid floating-point drift across languages; clients parse to float if needed.

pharmacological_classes.description: Controlled-vocabulary terms (often DrugBank “pharmacological class”) that power faceted search and ontology-driven UIs.

pharmacology.description: Paragraph-level bullets on absorption, distribution, metabolism, excretion and toxicity. Array keeps each bullet atomic for display.

pubchem_cid.description: Master external anchor for chemistry; every other cross-reference (SMILES, synonyms) resolves through this ID. (Denotes table primary key.)

pubchem_cid_i.description: Mirrors pubchem_cid as an integer for fast range/sort queries in Solr (strings sort lexically, not numerically).

synonyms.description: Brand names, old systematic names, abbreviations— boosts search recall and supports name harmonization. Case-insensitive matching mirrors antibiotic_name.

version.description: Auto-generated by Apache Solr; increments every time the row is changed so BV-BRC can detect conflicting writes and replicate the index safely.

date_inserted.description: ISO-8601 UTC datetime when the enzyme-class reference was first loaded into BV-BRC, enabling incremental update workflows.

date_modified.description: Updated whenever ec_description or go mappings are revised; clients compare this to cached copies to refresh only changed rows.

ec_description.description: Human-readable description associated with the Enzyme Commission (EC) number, imported from IUBMB/NC-IUBMB lists; displayed in pathway viewers and tool-tips.

ec_number.description: Primary key. Hierarchical Enzyme Commission identifier defining the biochemical reaction a catalyst performs (e.g. 1.1.1.27). Serves as the main join key between genomes, pathways and external databases.

go.description: Array of GO accessions that cross-reference the EC activity (molecular-function nodes); used for functional-enrichment analyses and GO term look-ups.

version.description: Auto-incremented by Apache Solr whenever the row is updated; guarantees optimistic locking and safe replication.

assay_results.description: One entry per immunological assay in the IEDB source, usually formatted “assayID: positive/negative (result)”. Shown in the epitope-detail pop-up, drives faceted filtering by outcome.

bcell_assays.description: Numeric string (for legacy type consistency) giving the number of B-cell assays reported for this epitope.

comments.description: Free-text notes providing caveats or special conditions (e.g. “linear peptide tested as synthetic 15-mer”). Stored as an array so each remark is atomic.

date_inserted.description: ISO-8601 UTC datetime when the epitope row entered BV-BRC.

date_modified.description: Updated whenever any field changes; helps incremental exports and cache invalidation.

end.description: 1-based inclusive amino-acid (or nucleotide) coordinate on the source protein where the epitope ends.

epitope_id.description: Stable BV-BRC identifier (usually the IEDB epitope ID). Guarantees uniqueness.

epitope_sequence.description: Exact peptide (or nucleotide) sequence recognised by the immune system.

epitope_type.description: Linear peptide, Discontinuous peptide, Glycan, Lipid, etc.; informs downstream visualisation.

host_name.description: List of scientific or common names for experimental hosts (mouse, human, ferret).

mhc_assays.description: Numeric string tally of MHC-class I/II binding assays.

organism.description: Scientific name of the pathogen protein containing the epitope.

protein_accession.description: GenBank/RefSeq accession of the source protein sequence.

protein_name.description: Functional description of the protein containing the epitope.

start.description: 1-based inclusive coordinate on the protein where the epitope begins.

taxon_id.description: Numeric ID of organism; supports taxon-scoped epitope searches.

taxon_lineage_ids.description: Semicolon-delimited ancestry IDs for organism; used to build taxon trees.

taxon_lineage_names.description: Parallel lineage names string for user-friendly display.

tcell_assays.description: Numeric string giving the number of T-cell activation/proliferation assays.

total_assays.description: Sum of all B-cell, T-cell, and MHC assays recorded for this epitope; integer for quick numeric filtering.

version.description: Auto-increments on every update, letting BV-BRC detect conflicting writes and replicate safely.

assay_group.description: Connects replicate or related assays within the same paper or experimental batch.

assay_id.description: Stable BV-BRC identifier for the assay (usually the IEDB assay ID).

assay_measurement.description: Raw measurement such as IC50, titer, or fluorescence units recorded for the assay.

assay_measurement_unit.description: Units corresponding to assay_measurement (µg/ml, IU, nM, etc.).

assay_method.description: Free-text or controlled terms like ELISA, ELISPOT, ICS, MHC ELISA.

assay_result.description: Positive, Negative, Intermediate, Inconclusive — used for filtering dashboards.

assay_type.description: B-cell, T-cell, MHC binding, MHC ligand elution, etc.

authors.description: Concise citation field shown in search results.

date_inserted.description: ISO-8601 UTC datetime when this assay row entered BV-BRC.

date_modified.description: Updated whenever any attribute changes; supports incremental syncs.

end.description: 1-based inclusive AA (or NT) coordinate on the source protein where the epitope ends.

epitope_sequence.description: Exact linear peptide (or nucleotide) sequence used in the assay.

epitope_type.description: Linear peptide, Discontinuous peptide, Lipid, etc.

host_name.description: Scientific or common name of the assay host (human donor, mouse strain, ferret, etc.).

host_taxon_id.description: Stored as string to accommodate missing values; enables host-specific queries.

mhc_allele.description: HLA or MHC designation (e.g. HLA-A02:01*). Blank for non-MHC assays.

mhc_allele_class.description: I, II, or I/II hybrid; helps stratify binding results.

organism.description: Scientific name of the organism providing the antigen.

pdb_id.description: Four-character code if the epitope is structurally mapped; empty otherwise.

pmid.description: Primary citation for the assay; blank if none.

protein_accession.description: Accession of the source protein sequence tested.

protein_name.description: Functional name displayed in assay tables.

start.description: 1-based inclusive coordinate where the epitope begins on the protein.

taxon_id.description: Numeric ID mirroring organism; used for taxon-scoped queries.

taxon_lineage_ids.description: Semicolon-delimited ancestry IDs for organism; feeds breadcrumb tree filters.

taxon_lineage_names.description: Parallel array of lineage names; improves human-readable display.

title.description: Short descriptive title from the source publication or IEDB record.

version.description: Auto-generated by Apache Solr; increments every time the row is edited so BV-BRC can detect conflicting writes and replicate safely.

date_inserted.description: ISO-8601 UTC datetime when the Gene Ontology (GO) term was first loaded into BV-BRC; lets ETL pipelines pull only new entries.

date_modified.description: Updated whenever go_name, definition or higher-level placement is changed; clients compare this to cached copies to decide whether to refresh.

definition.description: Text from the GO Consortium that precisely states the biological concept represented by the GO term; displayed in pop-up tool-tips and enrichment reports.

go_id.description: Primary key. Stable identifier with namespace prefix (GO:) followed by seven digits. Serves as join key between BV-BRC genome features, enzyme classes, and external GO resources.

go_name.description: Short human-readable label for the GO concept; shown in annotation tables and enrichment plots.

ontology.description: One of biological_process, molecular_function, or cellular_component; enables namespace-specific filtering and visualisation.

extra_query_examples.description: Query for sequences with taxon_id == 1313, limit to 5 sequences.  Return TSV data.

genome_name.description: Primary display name used in tables and downloads.

genome_status.description: Values such as Complete, Draft, WGS.

contigs.description: All sequence entries in the assembly.

version.description: Auto-generated by Apache Solr; increments on every update so BV-BRC can detect conflicting writes and replicate safely.

additional_metadata.description: Curated or user-supplied clinical / experimental tags that don’t map to fixed columns; exposed as searchable facets.

altitude.description: Elevation of collection site, with units if known; useful for ecological studies.

antimicrobial_resistance.description: Values such as Resistant, Susceptible, Intermediate for one or more drugs.

antimicrobial_resistance_evidence.description: Indicates whether the AMR value comes from Phenotype, AMR Panel, or Comment.

assembly_accession.description: Stable accession assigned by NCBI to the submitted assembly.

assembly_method.description: Free-text record of assembler name + version; displayed in sequence-quality reports.

authors.description: Comma-delimited list from the associated genome paper or GenBank record.

bioproject_accession.description: Links genome to its umbrella project record.

biosample_accession.description: Connects genome to detailed sample metadata in BioSample.

cds.description: Count of protein-coding genes annotated in the genome.

cds_ratio.description: CDS count divided by genome length (×1000); proxy for gene density.

cell_shape.description: Terms like coccus, rod, spirillum; supports phenotype filters.

checkm_completeness.description: Marker-gene based completeness estimate for MAGs / draft genomes.

clade.description: Used mainly for viral lineages (e.g. A(H1N1)pdm09).

collection_date.description: Raw text from metadata; may include ranges or partial dates.

comments.description: Additional remarks not captured in structured fields.

completion_date.description: When sequencing / assembly was declared finished.

contig_l50.description: Number of contigs whose summed length equals ≥50 % of genome.

contig_n50.description: Length of the shortest contig in the top 50 % by size.

core_families.description: Number of pan-genome protein families present in ≥95 % of a genus; helps gauge essential gene set coverage.

core_family_ratio.description: core_families divided by total PGFams for this genome.

county.description: Mid-level geographic tag for U.S. / UK style addresses.

date_inserted.description: When the genome row entered BV-BRC.

date_modified.description: Updates whenever any field changes.

depth.description: Environmental depth (soil core, water column) with units.

genbank_accessions.description: Comma-delimited GenBank accessions for all replicons.

genome_quality.description: One of Finished, High Quality Draft, Low Quality Draft, Metagenome Metagenome-Assembled Genome, etc.

h1_clade_global.description: One or more WHO/Nextstrain H1 global clade designations derived from the HA segment phylogeny.

h1_clade_us.description: USDA/IRMA H1 clade labels used for North-American swine surveillance.

h3_clade.description: Nextstrain/WHO clades for H3 influenza viruses.

h_type.description: Hemagglutinin subtype Hn (1 – 18); null for non-IAV genomes.

habitat.description: Broad environment where the organism lives (e.g. marine, soil, host-associated).

host_age.description: Age or age-range text pulled from BioSample (units vary).

host_group.description: Broad host class (human, avian, swine, equine, other mammal).

host_name.description: Raw text as provided by submitter; may mix name & strain.

host_scientific_name.description: NCBI-taxon scientific name; enables taxonomic lookup.

hypothetical_cds.description: Number of protein-coding genes without functional annotation.

isolation_comments.description: Curator or submitter comments about sampling context.

isolation_source.description: Text such as nasal swab, feces, soil, water.

kingdom.description: Derived from NCBI lineage; e.g. Bacteria, Viruses, Fungi.

lab_host.description: Cell line or animal used for passage.

lineage.description: Concise lineage string (B.1.1.7, 1/2a, etc.) or genotypic set for bacteria.

mat_peptide.description: Number of translated mature peptides (mainly viruses).

missing_core_family_ids.description: List of expected core families missing from the genome; aids MAG completeness review.

ncbi_project_id.description: Historical project identifier (now superseded by BioProject).

nearest_genomes.description: List of BV-BRC genome_ids within the “Similar Genome Finder” threshold.

optimal_temperature.description: Text or numeric range (°C) for culture growth.

organism_name.description: Extra strain designations or submitter-preferred name.

other_clinical.description: Flexible list of keywords (e.g. antibiotic use, ICU patient).

other_environmental.description: Describes abiotic factors like pH, heavy-metal load.

other_names.description: Helps fuzzy search on old nomenclature.

outgroup_genomes.description: Reference genomes used as outgroup in comparative trees.

owner.description: BV-BRC user or organization that controls the record.

p2_genome_id.description: Numeric genome ID from the retired PATRIC2 system; retained for cross-walks.

partial_cds.description: Number of coding sequences flagged as partial.

passage.description: Text like E3/M1 (egg / MDCK passage) or original.

patric_cds.description: Protein-coding genes predicted by the RASTtk pipeline.

ph1n1_like.description: “yes” if HA derives from 2009 pandemic lineage.

plasmids.description: Number of replicons flagged as plasmids.

plfam_cds.description: Gene count mapped to a PATRIC local family.

public.description: true if the genome is publicly viewable; false for private workspaces.

publication.description: PubMed ID or full citation for reference genome.

reference_genome.description: "Reference", "Representative", or empty; used in analysis defaults.

refseq_cds.description: Protein-coding gene count from NCBI annotation.

refseq_project_id.description: Identifier of the RefSeq genome project.

salinity.description: Text or numeric range (% or ppt).

season.description: Year/season string; common in influenza metadata.

segment.description: For segmented viruses (PB2, HA, NA, etc.).

sequencing_centers.description: Free-text list of institutions that produced the reads.

sequencing_depth.description: “×” value or range, often with platform.

sequencing_platform.description: Illumina MiSeq, Oxford Nanopore MinION, etc.

subtype.description: Influenza HxNy or other virus subtype text.

taxon_lineage_ids.description: Array of ancestor taxon IDs up to root.

temperature_range.description: Psychrophile, Mesophile, Thermophile or explicit °C range.

type_strain.description: “yes” if the isolate is the species type-strain.

user_read.description: BV-BRC user/org UUIDs permitted to view the record.

4_ha.description: Hemagglutinin (HA) GenBank IDs; key target for clade and subtype assignment.

collection_date.description: Free-text date from submitter (may include ranges or partial dates).

collection_year.description: Four-digit year extracted from collection_date for fast faceting.

date_inserted.description: ISO-8601 UTC date when the strain record entered BV-BRC.

date_modified.description: Updated on any field change; supports cache invalidation.

genbank_accessions.description: Full list of GenBank IDs across the segmented genome; convenience field for bulk download.

genome_ids.description: One or more genome_id values that belong to this strain (each segment is stored as a separate “genome” record).

host_common_name.description: Vernacular name of the host species.

host_group.description: Coarse category (avian, swine, human, equine, other mammal).

id.description: Stable BV-BRC strain identifier; often concatenation of host/geo/year.

l.description: For bunyaviruses/arenaviruses: accessions for L segment(s).

m.description: M-segment GenBank IDs for tri-segmented viruses.

other_segments.description: Any additional or unlabelled segments (bat IAV, defective RNAs).

passage.description: Text such as E2/M1 or original.

antibiotic.description: Canonical antibiotic name (e.g. CLSI preferred). Used for faceted search and AMR dashboards.

computational_method.description: Pipeline label such as AMRFinderPlus, ResFinder, RGI; enables provenance tracking when phenotype is predicted, not measured.

computational_method_performance.description: Precision/recall, F1, or other summary of the method on validation data; stored as free text.

computational_method_version.description: Semantic version or git hash; helps reproduce predictions.

date_modified.description: Updated whenever any field is edited; used by incremental export pipelines.

evidence.description: Controlled values: Phenotype, Genotype, Literature, Comment; indicates why the record exists.

testing_standard.description: CLSI, EUCAST, CDC, WHO—defines breakpoint table.

date_inserted.description: ISO-8601 UTC datetime when the sequence row entered BV-BRC. Used by ETL jobs and incremental export utilities.

date_modified.description: Updated whenever any field in the row changes (e.g. sequence correction). Lets clients refresh only modified records.

md5.description: 32-character hexadecimal MD5 digest of the raw sequence characters (uppercase FASTA, no gaps). Serves as the primary key, allowing rapid deduplication and cross-genome look-ups.

sequence.description: Full FASTA content (without header) for the feature: DNA for genes/RNAs, protein for CDS translations. Stored in compressed blocks; streamed on demand for downloads and BLAST queries.

sequence_type.description: Indicates whether sequence is dna, rna, or protein. Guides downstream tools to choose translation, alignment, or motif settings correctly.

aa_length.description: Number of amino-acid residues in the translated product of a CDS. Used in quality checks, alignment trimming, and size filters.

aa_sequence_md5.description: 32-char MD5 hash for the amino-acid sequence; enables rapid duplicate detection without shipping the full sequence.

accession.description: GenBank/RefSeq accession of the replicon on which the feature resides.

alt_locus_tag.description: Historic or secondary locus tag from earlier annotations; improves cross-version mapping.

annotation.description: Label such as PATRIC, RASTtk, RefSeq; lets users compare pipelines.

brc_id.description: Internal monotonically increasing integer (string-typed) uniquely identifying the feature across clusters.

classifier_round.description: Training round number used to generate the current classifier_score; supports auditing.

classifier_score.description: Confidence (0–1) that the CDS is a true protein-coding gene according to BV-BRC’s machine-learning model.

codon_start.description: 1, 2 or 3 value sent to GenBank / tbl2asn to mark the first coding frame relative to feature start.

date_modified.description: Updated any time an attribute (location, product name, etc.) changes; drives incremental exports.

feature_type.description: Controlled values: CDS, rRNA, tRNA, misc_feature, repeat_region, etc.

figfam_id.description: Protein family ID from legacy FIGfam scheme; enables compatibility with older PATRIC tools.

gene.description: Short locus name (recA, rpoB). Case-insensitive for search.

gene_id.description: Numeric GeneID from NCBI Gene when available.

genome_id.description: Foreign key linking to the genome metadata record.

go.description: List of Gene Ontology identifiers assigned to the protein; supports functional enrichment.

location.description: Concise PATRIC location format contig_start+len or contig_start-len (strand encoded by sign).

na_sequence_md5.description: Hash of the genomic DNA sequence underlying the feature; speeds up variant detection.

og_id.description: Pan-domain orthologous group (e.g. eggNOG) used for cross-kingdom analyses.

owner.description: BV-BRC user or group that controls write access.

p2_feature_id.description: Numeric key from retired schema; retained for backward compatibility.

patric_id.description: Historical alias identical to feature_id for CDS; kept to avoid breaking old APIs.

pdb_accession.description: List of matching PDB entries for the protein.

pgfam_id.description: Global protein family ID (cross-genus, length-normalized clustering).

plfam_id.description: Local (within-genus) protein family ID; finer granularity than PGFam.

product.description: Curated or predicted functional description; shown in browsers and BLAST.

protein_id.description: INSDC / RefSeq protein accession for the translated product.

refseq_locus_tag.description: Official NCBI locus tag for RefSeq annotation sets.

segments.description: For spliced genes: array of start..end regions; used by GFF exporters.

sequence_id.description: Links to genome_sequence.sequence_id (chromosome, plasmid, or viral segment).

sog_id.description: Finer split of og_id based on bidirectional best hits; helps detect recent duplications.

strand.description: "+" or "-"; required for translation and plotting.

taxon_id.description: Taxon of the parent genome; used for taxon-restricted searches.

uniprotkb_accession.description: Primary accession mapping to UniProt for functional & structural metadata.

id.description: Unique BV-BRC identifier for the mapping row, typically a concatenation <id_type>│<id_value> or an internal UUID.

id_value.description: The raw identifier string from the external database; used in API joins and export files.

uniprotkb_accession.description: When the external ID maps to a UniProt entry, this field stores the primary UniProtKB accession, enabling fast jumps to UniProt resources. Blank if not applicable.

version.description: Auto-generated by Apache Solr; increments whenever the row is edited so BV-BRC can detect write conflicts and replicate the index safely.

date_inserted.description: ISO-8601 UTC datetime when the NIAID SGC target row was first loaded into BV-BRC; lets ETL pipelines pull only new entries.

date_modified.description: Updated whenever any field in the row changes; clients use it to refresh only changed records.

gene_symbol_collection.description: One or more HGNC/NCBI gene symbols belonging to the same structural-genomics “target” (e.g. orthologues or paralogues selected for cloning/structural work).

genus.description: Latin genus of the organism from which the structural-genomics target originates; helps users filter by taxonomic breadth.

has_clones.description: yes, no, partial — indicates whether expression clones have been generated and are available through the NIAID Structural Genomics Center (SGC) repository.

has_proteins.description: yes, no, in_progress — shows whether soluble protein has been produced for downstream assays or crystallography.

selection_criteria.description: Short phrase explaining why the gene/family was chosen (e.g. “essential gene”, “novel drug target”, “immune evasion factor”).

species.description: Scientific binomial of the organism providing the gene/protein for the SGC pipeline.

strain.description: Specific strain used for cloning (important when gene sequence varies between strains).

target_id.description: Stable identifier assigned by the NIAID SGC to each structural-genomics target; used as join key to link clones, expression data and structures.

target_status.description: Current stage in the structural-genomics pipeline — values like selected, cloned, expressed, purified, crystallized, structure_determined. Guides users to what materials are available.

family_id.description: Primary key. Stable accession for the family. Two namespaces are common:• PGF_ IDs — “PATRIC Global Families” (cross-genus, 30 % AA ID clustering).• PLF_ IDs — “PATRIC Local Families” (within-genus, 50 % AA ID). Serves as the join key that links individual genome features to multiple comparative-genomics tools (alignments, heat-maps, phylogenies).

family_product.description: Curator-approved functional description summarising the consensus activity of the family’s members. Displayed in family browsers, alignment viewers, and annotation tables; helps users understand what a family “does”.

family_type.description: Indicates the clustering scheme:• PGFam = cross-genus global family (broad, coarse-grained).• PLFam = local within-genus family (finer resolution).Additional values such as FIGfam may appear for legacy sets. Used by UI filters and pipeline decisions (e.g. choose PGFams for genus-wide core analysis, PLFams for strain-level comparison).
